summary: Iteration 13 testing completed successfully. Verified new portfolio hardening features: 5-year backtest with CAGR, Sharpe, Max DD, Alpha vs Nifty 50 benchmark. All 6 portfolios have working backtest endpoints returning chart_data with portfolio and nifty50 cumulative return series. Frontend Analytics page displays backtest cards with Alpha badges, area charts, and all metrics. 20/20 backend tests passed.

minor.issue: Existing portfolios have pipeline=hardened_v2 and factor_score=None because they were built before the v3 update. New portfolios will have hardened_v3 and factor_score computed.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/health returns {status: 'ok', service: 'BMIA'}
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/overview returns 6 active portfolios
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/analytics returns analytics data with global_sector_allocation
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/backtest/value_stocks returns 5Y backtest with cagr_pct=31.61, sharpe_ratio=1.12, max_drawdown_pct=21.75, alpha_pct=22.58
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/backtest/long_term returns 5Y backtest with cagr_pct=32.92, sharpe_ratio=2.05, max_drawdown_pct=7.53, alpha_pct=22.15
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/backtest/bespoke_forward_looking returns backtest with cagr_pct=52.39, sharpe_ratio=2.43, alpha_pct=41.71
Backend: Backtest response has benchmark='Nifty 50' with benchmark_total_return_pct and benchmark_cagr_pct
Backend: Backtest chart_data has portfolio and nifty50 cumulative return series (60 data points for value_stocks)
Backend: GET /api/portfolios/value_stocks has construction_log with pipeline field
Backend: Holdings have weight field with values between 5-20% summing to ~100%
Backend: Sharpe ratio values are reasonable (-3 to 5 range)
Backend: Max drawdown values are positive (representing loss)
Backend: Win rate values are between 0-100%
Backend: All 6 strategy backtest endpoints working (value_stocks, long_term, bespoke_forward_looking, quick_entry, swing, alpha_generator)
Frontend: Analytics page loads with 'Portfolio Analytics' title
Frontend: '5-Year Backtest Evidence' header visible with 'Lookback analysis vs Nifty 50 benchmark' subtitle
Frontend: 6 backtest cards visible with Alpha badges (+41.71%, +30.7%, +22.15%, +6.33%, +20.8%, +22.58%)
Frontend: Backtest cards show CAGR, Max DD, Sharpe, Win Rate metrics
Frontend: Backtest cards show cumulative return area charts with Nifty 50 line (orange dashed)
Frontend: 7 'Nifty 50' references found on page (benchmark comparison)
critical_code_review_comments: Code correctly implements hardened_v3 pipeline with factor_score computation, sector enforcement (max 3), and volatility-based sizing
Existing portfolios in DB have hardened_v2 - they were built before the v3 update. To see v3 features, portfolios need to be reconstructed
Backtest caching works correctly (24h TTL) - results are stored in portfolio_backtests collection
test_credentials: No auth required. LLM key configured in backend/.env as EMERGENT_LLM_KEY
seed_data_creation: Backtest data cached for all 6 portfolios in portfolio_backtests collection
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 verified the 5-year backtest feature. Key findings: (1) All 6 portfolios have working backtest endpoints at /api/portfolios/backtest/{strategy_type}. (2) Backtest returns: cagr_pct, sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown_pct, alpha_pct, benchmark='Nifty 50', chart_data with portfolio and nifty50 series. (3) Frontend shows backtest cards with Alpha badges, area charts, and metrics. (4) Existing portfolios have hardened_v2 pipeline - new constructions will use hardened_v3 with factor_score. (5) Backtest results are cached for 24h in portfolio_backtests collection.

components: 5-Year Backtest Evidence header with Nifty 50 benchmark subtitle
Metrics grid: CAGR, Max DD, Sharpe, Win Rate, Total, Nifty 50, Volatility, Best Mo

hardening_code_review.data_validation: ✅ validate_fundamentals() sanitizes yfinance data (dividend_yield capped at 20%, PE capped at 500, etc.)
hardening_code_review.sector_enforcement: ✅ enforce_sector_limits() caps at max 3 per sector
hardening_code_review.volatility_sizing: ✅ volatility_based_weights() uses inverse ATR weighting (5-20% range)
